destinations.evidence: Obligation synthesis derives due execution indices from Scheduler state, bounds them by num_calls, and skips already executed indices.
destinations.snippet: due_index_bounds clamps max_due_index to num_calls - 1; compute_obligations emits ScheduledObligation { call_id, index } only for unexecuted due indices.

destinations.evidence: Each obligation becomes exactly one TxScheduled carrying the same call_id and execution index in calldata and metadata.
destinations.snippet: executeCall { callId: U256::from(obligation.call_id), executionIndex: U256::from(obligation.index) }; TxScheduled::new_with_options(... obligation.call_id, obligation.index, ...).

destinations.evidence: Payload building deduplicates scheduled work by (call_id, execution_index).
destinations.snippet: let mut included_scheduled_indices = HashSet::<(u64, u64)>::new(); ... contains/insert key

destinations.evidence: Verifier rejects duplicate, out-of-bounds, and already-executed scheduled indices.

equivalence_argument: Structural mapping from old canonical per-index scheduling to v2 parent-state obligation synthesis: due indices are bounded, materialized once into TxScheduled, deduplicated in payload building, and rejected by the verifier if duplicate/out-of-bounds/replayed.

related_but_insufficient_code: Old crates/scheduling/src/canonical.rs is absent; live owner is scheduled verification/block-building/payload code.

rationale: The packet contains one high-confidence canonical scheduling candidate. Neighbor candidates provide the surrounding scheduled transaction pipeline, but none supersede the duplicate-prevention rule that canonical processing gates submission by current execution index and fired count.
statement: Canonical scheduling processing must submit a scheduled transaction only when the current execution index equals the call's current fired count, and it must not emit more than num_calls executions for that call.
suggested_falsification_tests: For a call with start_block S, frequency F, and num_calls N, process canonical blocks across multiple periods and assert exactly one transaction is submitted for each execution index 0 through N-1.
Reprocess or duplicate a block for an already-fired index and process blocks after N executions; assert no duplicate or out-of-bounds scheduled transaction is submitted.
summary: One scheduled transaction per execution index is preserved through obligation synthesis, payload deduplication, and verifier rejection.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create Scheduler storage with start/frequency/num_calls and executed bitmap, then assert only unexecuted due indices < num_calls are emitted and duplicates/out-of-bounds/replays are rejected.
title: One scheduled tx per execution index
